blurb: Paul lifts a runaway slave and says: he is no longer property, he is my own heart. In that moment the gospel quietly topples an economy—relationships become dearer than debts, persons outrank roles. We are invited, still, to look across every boundary we have drawn and dare to call the once-excluded “brother, sister, heart.”

verses.text: I beseech thee for my son Onesimus, whom I have begotten in my bonds:
Which in time past was to thee unprofitable, but now profitable to thee and to me:
Whom I have sent again: thou therefore receive him, that is, mine own bowels:
Whom I would have retained with me, that in thy stead he might have ministered unto me in the bonds of the gospel:
